message:
feat(workspace): extract frankenterm-core-replay-types leaf crate (ft-j1qjt.1)

Carves replay_decision_graph (1032 lines, leaf-clean) out of
frankenterm-core into a new frankenterm-core-replay-types leaf crate.
Same pattern as ft-usvnt (resource-types), ft-g6sa8 (error-types), etc.

Why this slice:
  - The full ft-j1qjt.1 ambition was to extract all 7 types core
    depends on from the replay cluster (SharedCaptureAdapter,
    CaptureAdapter, CaptureConfig, CollectingCaptureSink, plus
    DecisionEvent / DecisionType / FtreplayArtifact). Audit found
    that 3 of those (the *Adapter types) carry a RecorderEventSource
    field which is itself in recording.rs — extracting them requires
    extracting RecorderEventSource first. Filed as ft-j1qjt.1.1
    (auto-numbered ft-j1qjt.3).
  - DecisionEvent + DecisionType actually live in
    replay_decision_graph.rs (not replay_capture.rs — the latter just
    re-exports them). That file had ZERO crate:: deps (one inline
    reference to crate::replay_capture::summarize_decision_input,
    inlined here). So replay_decision_graph IS leaf-clean and can
    extract immediately.

Mechanics:
  - Created crates/frankenterm-core-replay-types/ with serde +
    serde_json as the only dependencies.
  - mv'd replay_decision_graph.rs to the new crate.
  - Inlined the body of summarize_decision_input (8 lines of UTF-8
    boundary handling) into the leaf rather than introducing a
    back-edge to core.
  - frankenterm-core/src/lib.rs: replaced
    `pub mod replay_decision_graph;` with
    `pub use frankenterm_core_replay_types::replay_decision_graph;`
    so existing `crate::replay_decision_graph::*` and
    `frankenterm_core::replay_decision_graph::*` paths keep
    resolving.
  - replay_capture.rs's existing
    `pub use crate::replay_decision_graph::{DecisionEvent, DecisionType};`
    now resolves through the re-export chain.
  - Workspace member added; frankenterm-core dep wired.

Verified: cargo check -p frankenterm-core-replay-types -p
frankenterm-core clean.

Closes ft-j1qjt.1. Filed ft-j1qjt.3 (= ft-j1qjt.1.1) for the
RecorderEventSource extraction that unblocks the next slice.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-0etp3","title":"[MEDIUM] SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_VERSION/DIMENSION constants are hardcoded placeholders — invalidation wiring is dead (821edfad smell)","description":"821edfad introduced schema-metadata invalidation but SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_VERSION="unversioned" and SEMANTIC_EMBEDDING_DIMENSION=0 at chunk_vector_store.rs:28-30 are compile-time placeholders. The invalidation logic never fires because the constants never change. Plumb real embedder identity through (fastembed model name + dim) so the mechanism is live.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:29:16.609336Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:15:25.630968Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:15:25.630290Z","close_reason":"d5198489 replaces the dead placeholder constants SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_VERSION='unversioned' / SEMANTIC_EMBEDDING_DIMENSION=0 with a real SemanticEmbedderIdentity struct (embedder_version + embedding_dimension) and plumbs it through a new ChunkVectorStore::open_with_identity(path, identity) entry point. ensure_semantic_store_metadata now consults the runtime identity instead of compile-time constants, so the invalidation gate actually fires when the embedder behind the store changes. Identity is feature-flag-agnostic — the core crate does not import fastembed; callers use SemanticEmbedderIdentity::from_fastembed_pipeline(name, dim) as the bridge. Legacy ChunkVectorStore::open(path) preserved as a thin wrapper around open_with_identity(path, unversioned()) — pinned by a regression test. Five new tests: (1) flipping_embedder_version_invalidates (same dim, different model name), (2) flipping_embedding_dimension_invalidates (same name, 384→768), (3) reopening_with_same_identity_is_a_noop, (4) legacy_open_uses_unversioned_identity, (5) semantic_embedder_identity_default_is_unversioned. SemanticEmbedderIdentity exported from crate::search for future recorder/orchestrator wiring. 66 chunk_vector_store tests pass via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target including the existing ft-xphjm atomicity tests against the new identity-aware signature.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","review-of-review","search"]}
 {"id":"ft-0p5q5","title":"[MEDIUM] ars_drift::DriftMonitor::observe NaN-poisons null_rate and silently prevents drift detection","description":"# Defect

## File + line

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/ars_drift.rs:162` — `DriftMonitor::observe`.

## Concrete failure path

```rust
pub fn observe(&mut self, outcome: f64, config: &EValueConfig) -> DriftVerdict {
    self.total_observations += 1;
    let outcome = outcome.clamp(0.0, 1.0);  // ← NaN-bypasses: f64::clamp(NaN, _, _) returns NaN
    ...
}
```

Same underlying gotcha as ft-761tz (disk_pressure) and ft-b4l62 (telemetry Histogram): `f64::clamp` returns NaN for NaN input. One NaN `outcome` then taints:

1. `self.calibration.push(NaN)` — NaN enters the calibration Vec.
2. `sum: f64 = self.calibration.iter().sum()` → NaN (NaN is absorbing under addition).
3. `self.null_rate = sum / n` → NaN.
4. `self.null_rate.clamp(0.01, 0.99)` → NaN (same gotcha).
5. `calibrated = true` with a NaN `null_rate` — permanent poison.
6. Every post-calibration observe():
   * `factor = 1.0 + lambda * (outcome - null_rate)` → NaN.
   * `factor.max(0.0)` → 0.0 (f64::max ignores NaN).
   * `e_value = e_value * decay * 0.0 = 0.0`.
   * `e_value >= threshold` is false forever.
   * `DriftVerdict::NoDrift` returned for every sample.

**Net: one NaN outcome silently disables drift detection for the remaining lifetime of the monitor.** Operators trust 'no drift' reports; the monitor is effectively a no-op.

## Severity rationale

- Silent wrong answer — the worst possible disposition for an observability/drift detector.
- Not a crash, not data loss.
- Recovery requires `full_reset()` — which nobody calls automatically since the monitor appears healthy.
- Same class as disk_pressure (ft-761tz), rate_limit (ft-7ojbj), backpressure config (ft-atcr0). The swarm has been closing NaN gotchas systematically; this is the next one.

## Fix sketch

`observe(NaN)` must be a no-op: do not bump `total_observations`, do not push into calibration, do not poison state. Return the current verdict (typically NoDrift or InsufficientData depending on phase).

```rust
pub fn observe(&mut self, outcome: f64, config: &EValueConfig) -> DriftVerdict {
    if outcome.is_nan() {
        // Surface the current state rather than poisoning the monitor.
        return if !self.calibrated {
            DriftVerdict::InsufficientData {
                observations: self.calibration.len(),
                required: config.min_calibration,
            }
        } else {
            DriftVerdict::NoDrift {
                e_value: self.e_value,
                null_rate: self.null_rate,
            }
        };
    }
    self.total_observations += 1;
    let outcome = outcome.clamp(0.0, 1.0);
    ...
}
```

Also null_rate.clamp(0.01, 0.99) after mean computation is itself NaN-vulnerable — defense in depth: if for any reason null_rate is NaN at that point (future code change), snap to the midpoint 0.5 rather than letting NaN propagate. Not strictly needed with the entry guard above, but cheap.

## Tests

* `ft_0p5q5_observe_nan_does_not_enter_calibration` — NaN input leaves `total_observations` unchanged, calibration Vec unchanged, returns InsufficientData.
* `ft_0p5q5_observe_nan_after_calibration_preserves_verdict` — NaN input after calibration returns NoDrift without touching e_value or null_rate.
* `ft_0p5q5_observe_nan_then_clean_sample_detects_drift` — end-to-end: NaN then clean sequence still triggers drift when threshold crossed (no permanent poison).

## Scope

Single file (`ars_drift.rs`). No public API break — `observe(NaN)` becomes a no-op which matches the fail-closed precedent.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:00:27.594084Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:04:54.105196Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:04:54.104932Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-0s1os","title":"[reality-gap] README tx examples target a top-level CLI that the shipped ft binary does not expose","description":"README.md documents top-level `ft tx ...` commands in the command matrix and troubleshooting examples, but the shipped binary at `FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft` rejects `ft tx --help` with `error: unrecognized subcommand 'tx'`.

Observed reality:
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft --help` has no top-level `tx` command.
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft robot --help` advertises `tx plan|run|rollback|show` under robot mode.
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft mission plan --contract mission.json` also fails because the live flag is `--mission-file`, not `--contract`.

Impact: an operator following README examples gets an immediate parse failure from the shipped binary.

Expected fix:
- Update README tx examples to the live robot surface (`ft robot tx ...`).
- Use the live flag spelling (`--contract-file`) where a contract path example is shown.
- If a top-level human tx surface is intended, ship it and re-verify the binary before documenting it.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","created_by":"GreenWren","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:34:07Z","close_reason":"Fixed by README docs correction in the same session: tx examples now point at `ft robot tx ...`, and the contract-path example now uses `--contract-file`.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","reality-gap"]}
 {"id":"ft-0v48y","title":"[HIGH] FrankenSqlite recorder backend is a stub — config-selecting it fails at bootstrap (recorder_storage.rs:577)","description":"Agent A mock-code-finder: FrankenSqlite recorder backend returns BackendUnavailable; only AppendLog is wired. If config.storage.recorder_backend=frankensqlite, bootstrap fails cleanly but the feature surface is entirely missing. Cross-ref: config.rs:879 rejects same value at config load. Either (a) mark frankensqlite backend as 'planned' in docs and in the config parser (hide the variant), or (b) implement the backend (see wa-2l27x.8 FrankenSQLite migration plan).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:50:10.136429Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:53:49.916655Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:53:49.916340Z","close_reason":"Shipped in fe0e2ca3: default builds reject frankensqlite recorder selection behind non-default feature gate","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","mock","storage"]}
 {"id":"ft-0vt3k","title":"[SEV] full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology rollback check only compares pane id set, not split geometry","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/tests/integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs full_pipeline_mux_checkpoint_topology Phase 5 (rewritten in 30172171 to fix ft-olq2e) now derives post_rollback_pane_ids from server.registry().snapshot() and asserts the sorted id list matches baseline_pane_ids. This is stronger than the previous vacuous baseline-JSON roundtrip, but it still only validates the *set* of surviving pane ids, not the restored split tree geometry: split ratios, split directions (horizontal vs vertical), parent/child edges, pane widths/heights, or the z-order of panes within the topology. A broken rollback that restores the correct 2 pane ids but swaps their split direction, scales their sizes, or re-parents them under a different split node would still pass this assertion. Metamorphic invariant: TopologySnapshot::from_registry(post_rollback) == TopologySnapshot::from_registry(pre_expansion) — not currently asserted. Fix by capturing a full TopologySnapshot before Phase 3 expansion, then after rollback compare structural fields (split tree, ratios, per-pane dimensions) in addition to pane id set. The existing 'restored_topo = from_json(&initial_topo_json)' on line 422 still only proves serde roundtrip; it does not compare to the live post-rollback state.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:42.691288Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:28:05.912783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:28:05.912477Z","close_reason":"Resolved in 4784c64f + d29498d6. d29498d6 added TopologyFingerprint covering HSplit/VSplit direction, per-split child ratios (encode_ratio to u16), per-leaf pane_id/rows/cols, tab_id, active_pane_id, window_id, and active_tab_index; Phase 5 now asserts post_rollback_fingerprint == initial_fingerprint AND != expanded_fingerprint, so a rollback that restores the correct pane id set but swaps split direction, rescales panes, or re-parents nodes fails. 4784c64f tightened the check further with a direct TopologySnapshot equality (post_rollback_topo == &initial_topo) as the primary assertion and baseline-JSON == initial_topo as the secondary. Ran the test at HEAD: binary compiles clean; runtime verification on local host blocked by macOS dyld bottleneck under concurrent-agent compile load.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -256,8 +256,9 @@
 {"id":"ft-ih4tm","title":"[MEDIUM] defer_fts_triggers=false is not reversible: triggers stay dropped after enabling once (ft-wk5fo follow-up)","description":"# Defect

## Concrete failure path

File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs`.

d74401c6 landed `StorageConfig.defer_fts_triggers` (ft-wk5fo). The intent is a reversible opt-in: flip to `true` to trade FTS freshness for write-path latency, flip back to `false` to restore synchronous indexing. The inline comment at line 6006 says as much:

> The triggers can be re-created by re-opening with `defer_fts_triggers: false`

That claim is wrong. Concrete interleaving:

1. Operator opens DB with `defer_fts_triggers: true` on an up-to-date schema (`user_version == SCHEMA_VERSION`).
2. `initialize_schema` returns early at line 4315 (no SQL executed, up-to-date path).
3. The explicit `DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS output_segments_ai/ad/au` at line 6010 runs — triggers gone.
4. Operator flips config to `defer_fts_triggers: false` and restarts.
5. `initialize_schema` again short-circuits at line 4315.
6. Line 6010 is skipped (flag is false). **No `CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS` runs.**
7. Database silently stays in deferred mode; the operator's `false` setting is a no-op.

The inline comment at line 6007 hints the author knew: 'this commit ships the opt-in direction only; reverting would require re-running SCHEMA_SQL or the operator dropping the DB.' That matches reality but contradicts the line-6006 'can be re-created by re-opening' claim — and leaves the config behaving non-intuitively for operators.

## Severity rationale

- Not a crash or data loss.
- Silent wrong answer: operator flips back to `false`, expects synchronous FTS, gets deferred.
- One-way-door config is a known anti-pattern. Easy fix.

## Fix sketch

Make the open path truly bidirectional. On every open, after `initialize_schema` returns:

* If `defer_fts_triggers == true`: `DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS ...` (idempotent).
* If `defer_fts_triggers == false`: `CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS ...` using the same SQL the SCHEMA_SQL block defines.

Factor the trigger SQL into one private const referenced by both branches so SCHEMA_SQL and the re-create branch cannot drift.

## Tests

* ft_ih4tm_defer_fts_triggers_roundtrip — open with true, verify triggers dropped, close, reopen with false, verify triggers re-created.
* ft_ih4tm_defer_fts_triggers_idempotent_reopen — open with same flag twice back-to-back, no spurious SQL errors, trigger state stable.

## Scope

Single file (`storage.rs`). No API break (`defer_fts_triggers` already public, default `false`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:27:12.433144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:31:50.095316Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:31:50.095017Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-ismux","title":"[mission][dispatch] MissionDispatcher reports accepted dispatch without calling WorkflowRunner::handle_detection","description":"In `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs:200-226`, `dispatch_single()` builds a synthetic detection, then only calls `runner.find_matching_workflow(&detection)` and fabricates `accepted: true` plus a synthetic `execution_id` when a workflow exists. It never invokes `WorkflowRunner::handle_detection()`, so no lock acquisition, no concurrency-limit enforcement, no storage row, and no real workflow start happen.

Impact: callers receive a success-shaped `DispatchResult` that claims work was accepted even though the runner was never asked to start anything. This also makes the existing close reason on `ft-y9lnb.3` overstated relative to the current code.

Fix direction: bridge the sync mission loop to the async runner start path with a bounded runtime, map the actual `WorkflowStartResult` into `DispatchResult`, and only emit started metadata from a real start result.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:05:13.673841Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:49.575203Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:49.574685Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of ft-4r2ib. crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs is already dirty in this checkout with the exact handle_detection/WorkflowStartResult fix and regression coverage, so I did not collide with pane-4-owned WIP.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"ft-j1qjt.3","title":"[ft-j1qjt.1.1] extract RecorderEventSource from recording.rs to enable replay_capture leaf extraction","description":"Follow-up to ft-j1qjt.1: completing the replay-types leaf to also house replay_capture's CaptureAdapter/CaptureConfig/CollectingCaptureSink/SharedCaptureAdapter requires extracting RecorderEventSource (currently in recording.rs) into a leaf crate first because CaptureAdapter and CaptureConfig embed RecorderEventSource as a field. Once recording's source-classification enum lives in a leaf, the replay_capture types can move alongside replay_decision_graph in frankenterm-core-replay-types.  {"id":"ft-j3ayu","title":"[LOW] Top-level repo clutter not in AGENTS.md workspace structure / .gitignore (test_*.rs, ubs_*.txt, storage.sqlite3*, clippy_output.json)","description":"Reality-check audit F9: dozens of scratch files at repo root — test_glob.rs, test_hll.rs, test_policy.rs, ubs_core.txt 38KB, ubs_handlers.txt 6MB, storage.sqlite3 34MB + .bak 2.5MB, check_out.json, clippy_output.json.  {"id":"ft-yav06","title":"[review][LOW] wa.events conformance harness covers 1 event / 1 filter combo; no pagination, no empty-result, no filter-exclusion","description":"commit 01d5e233 landed crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_events_mcp_conformance.rs as the ft-4o5mb.2 first pass. The harness is CORRECT for its scope — single event, all 4 filters (pane+rule_id+label+unhandled) combined, one invalid-args boundary case. But the 'conformance' name implies broader coverage that the one test doesn't deliver.

Specific gaps a follow-up pass should close:

1. Filter CORRECTNESS, not just filter PASS-THROUGH. The current test seeds ONE event that matches all filters, then asserts the event appears. If the wa.events tool silently dropped a filter (e.g., rule_id filter was a no-op), this test would still pass because the seeded event matches all possible filters. Seed 3-4 events where some match and some don't, then assert only the matching ones come back. This is the test shape that actually fences the filter SQL.

2. Pagination / limit clamping. Test uses limit:10 with 1 event. Seeding 25 events with limit:10 would verify that limit actually caps results at 10, total_count reflects the full 25, and the returned subset is the most-recent-first (or whatever the documented order is).

3. Empty result. storage with 0 events + any wa.events call should return data:{events:[], total_count:0, ...}. Not asserted.

4. Multi-event ordering. The response order is undocumented from the test. Crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs query probably does ORDER BY detected_at DESC or id DESC — test should pin this.

5. Upper-bound invalid: test covers limit:0 rejection. limit:1001 (schema maximum:1000) should also reject. Not tested.

6. FT-MCP-0001 tool-level rejection at limit-bounds check (mcp_tools.rs:1770-1784) is DEAD CODE per this test (framework schema validation catches first). Either a test should exercise this path (malicious client bypassing schema) or the duplicate tool-level check should be documented as defense-in-depth.

Naming note: ft-4o5mb.2 subtask numbering implies this is deliberately a first pass. This bead documents the gaps for the next ft-4o5mb.2.x or follow-up bead to close.

Severity: LOW — not a bug, just incomplete coverage of a surface labeled 'conformance'. Discovered during reviewer audit round 10, 2026-04-23.

Tag: review, mcp, conformance, coverage.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:01:05.984563Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:07:02.144287Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:07:02.143427Z","close_reason":"Closed 4 of the 6 gaps in 1eb4a665. Added: (1) empty_storage test asserting events:[] + total_count:0; (2) rule_id_filter_excludes_non_matching — seeds 3 events with 3 different rule_ids, queries for one, asserts exactly one returned (fences rule_id SQL from becoming a no-op); (3) limit_caps_results_and_orders_newest_first — seeds 5, queries limit:3, asserts len=3, total_count=3 (matches mcp_tools.rs:1812 set-to-events.len semantics), detected_at monotonically DESC (pins storage.rs:16668 ORDER BY); (4) rejects_limit_above_schema_maximum — limit:1001 fails with 'root.limit: value must be <= 1000' (fastmcp schema.rs:535 format). Not in this pass (documented in commit message): defense-in-depth FT-MCP-0001 at mcp_tools.rs:1770-1784 (same open question as ft-tczj7 / wa-nu4.3.9.19), triage_state filter, since filter, multi-pane isolation. Scope held to the single reserved file.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-ybtyg","title":"[MEDIUM] session checkpoint + snapshot files have no integrity check; 'BLAKE3' docs vs SipHash reality","description":"Found during /security-audit-for-saas pass #5 (pane 5, 2026-04-21).

## Summary
Two related gaps in the session-persistence trust story:

1. Snapshot files persisted to SQLite (`session_checkpoints`,
   `mux_pane_state`) have NO integrity check on load. An attacker
   with DB write access — or a malicious snapshot bundle imported
   via diag tooling — can forge arbitrary checkpoint rows, and the
   restore path will replay them. The restored-command injection
   vector closed by ft-kegvt (commit 969b9e02) is one consequence;
   the root cause is that nothing cryptographically binds a
   checkpoint to a trusted writer.

2. Documentation drift amplifies the problem: snapshot_engine.rs:14
   advertises "BLAKE3 hash → dedup (skip if unchanged)" while the
   actual helper `compute_state_hash` at
   snapshot_engine.rs:1641-1651 uses
   `std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher` (SipHash-24, not a
   cryptographic hash). Even if the dedup-only role makes SipHash
   adequate for THAT purpose, the README/inline doc claim misleads
   future auditors into thinking integrity is enforced.

## Evidence

### Missing integrity verification
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:466-474 and
  :509-517: `INSERT INTO session_checkpoints (..., state_hash,
  ...)` with the literal string `'restore'` as the state_hash
  column value. No hash is computed, stored, or later verified.
- storage.rs:609 schema comment claims `state_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
  -- BLAKE3 of serialized state for dedup`, but the INSERT sites
  (:2031, :2268, :2589) either pass a computed SipHash hex string
  (from `compute_state_hash`) or the hardcoded `'restore'`.
- grep confirms zero `verify_snapshot`, `snapshot_hash`,
  `hmac`, `HMAC`, or `verify.*checkpoint` sites across
  `snapshot_engine.rs` + `session_restore.rs`. Load paths read
  `state_hash` for dedup-skip ONLY, never for integrity check.

### Documentation drift
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:14 — the module
  docstring says:
      `├── BLAKE3 hash  → dedup (skip if unchanged)`
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:1641-1651:
      ```rust
      fn compute_state_hash(panes: &[PaneInfo]) -> String {
          use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};
          let mut hasher =
              std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();
          ...
          ids.hash(&mut hasher);
          ...
          format!("{:016x}", hasher.finish())
      }
      ```
  That's SipHash-24 formatted as a 16-hex-char u64. Not BLAKE3.
  No `blake3::` / `sha2::` imports in snapshot_engine.rs.

## Impact / trust boundary
Integrity-less checkpoint storage means that any path which ends
up writing to the SQLite DB or shipping a DB file between hosts
(session bundle export/import, diag bundle with session data,
backup→restore, snapshot migration between workspaces) has no way
to detect tampering.

The ft-kegvt fix sanitizes CR/LF/ESC/C0 at the `launch_agent`
sanitizer boundary, which blunts the most dangerous class of
tamper-payload. But it doesn't stop an attacker from, e.g.,
forging checkpoint rows that silently restore into the wrong
workspace, swap window/tab ids to masquerade as a different
session, or ship tampered pane metadata for future replays.

Severity: MEDIUM. Needs DB write access or bundle-import trust
crossing — same class as ft-kegvt. Worth closing proactively
before snapshot import is exposed over any wider surface.

## Fix direction

1. Replace the dedup hash with a real BLAKE3 implementation
   (there's already a `blake3` dependency pulled in via
   `storage.rs` + `codec`). Keep the dedup role but also use the
   hash as the integrity witness on restore.
2. Drop the hardcoded `'restore'` placeholder at session_restore.rs:
   467, 510 — either compute a real state_hash over the
   PaneIdMap or make the column `TEXT NULL` and document why the
   restore-time insert has no pane state to hash yet.
3. On load, recompute the hash over the deserialized snapshot and
   compare to the stored value; fail loudly on mismatch with an
   actionable error ("snapshot integrity check failed: stored
   hash X, recomputed Y — refusing to restore").
4. For cross-host snapshot bundles (future), add an HMAC keyed
   from a workspace-local secret in `.ft/hmac.key` so bundles
   cannot be silently swapped between workspaces.
5. Fix the docstring drift at snapshot_engine.rs:14 in lockstep
   with whichever real hash gets adopted.

## Non-findings verified in the same pass
- MCP bearer token validation: no bearer surface in stdio MCP
  (mcp_bridge.rs). Auth is out-of-band via OS process context.
  Clean.
- `wa.send` approval enforcement in non-TTY: mcp_tools.rs:1930-1955
  properly handles `InjectionResult::RequiresApproval` — injection
  gated on `.is_allowed()` at line 1955. Approval-required actions
  return the envelope back, do not auto-bypass. Clean.
- Distributed mode token validation: `constant_time_eq` at
  distributed.rs:209-222 is correctly implemented. `validate_token`
  at :248-289 uses it for both identity and secret. Clean.
- Agent-mail contact handshake: out of scope per AGENTS.md rule
  (NEVER touch agent-mail service). Service-enforced; client
  bridge has no local contact-approval logic to audit. Out of
  scope by policy.

## Related
- ft-kegvt (969b9e02) — closed the CR/LF injection vector that
  could ride a tampered `command` field through the restore path.
  This bead is the structural fix; ft-kegvt was the sanitizer at
  the exit boundary.
- ft-security-mcp-path-traversal (30988e53) — arbitrary-file R/W
  via MCP; same trust-boundary-crossing theme.
- ft-xv561 (070117a0) — ReDoS via user-pack regexes.

Filed per /security-audit-for-saas pass #5.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:37:13.512571Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:42:52.630551Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:42:52.630257Z","close_reason":"Closed by 598a7c13. Replaced hardcoded 'restore' literal at session_restore.rs:467 (save_restore_checkpoint) and :557 (finalize_restore) with real compute_restore_state_hash(session_id, pane_id_map, now_ms) — SipHash-24 formatted as 16-hex-char u64. Domain separator ft-ybtyg:restore-checkpoint-v1 prevents collision with snapshot_engine dedup hash. Docstrings at snapshot_engine.rs:14 and storage.rs:609 realigned from misleading 'BLAKE3' to truthful 'SipHash-24 (not cryptographic integrity)'. 8 new regression tests cover determinism, shape, discrimination on session_id/map/ts, insertion-order independence, and domain separation. On-disk format preserved. Full load-time verification wiring is documented as follow-up since it requires coordinating with the existing dedup-skip semantics that also read state_hash.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","integrity","security","snapshot"]}
 {"id":"ft-ych74","title":"[review][LOW] release.yml smoke test excludes windows-latest — ft-windows-amd64.exe ships without --version/--help/doctor probe","description":".github/workflows/release.yml:68-80 gates the Smoke test binary step with:

    if: matrix.target == 'x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu' || startsWith(matrix.target, 'aarch64-apple') || startsWith(matrix.target, 'x86_64-apple')

The windows-latest / x86_64-pc-windows-msvc target (matrix entry at :48-51) is deliberately skipped. That matters because the smoke step is the only place the workflow exercises:

    "$BINARY" --version
    "$BINARY" version --full
    "$BINARY" --help
    "$BINARY" doctor --json

Any Windows-specific regression (path separator bug, ANSI/console handling, missing crate feature for msvc, clap rendering) ships to users because the release step (:151 action-gh-release) uploads ft-windows-amd64.exe without ever invoking it.

aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu is also skipped (the condition only matches x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu for linux), so the linux/arm64 binary is equally unexercised — two gaps, not one.

Fix: drop the if: guard entirely (smoke test runs on every matrix row) OR extend the condition to include aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu and x86_64-pc-windows-msvc, adjusting the shell to pwsh on windows (Compress-Archive uses pwsh already at :93 so the pattern exists).

Severity: LOW — pre-launch for v0.1.0 where no Windows users exist yet, but absent a smoke check the first Windows release can ship broken and only surface via install.sh failures in the field.

Tag: review, release. Discovered during reviewer audit round 5, 2026-04-23 while verifying 70d30504 release workflow audit — pane 5 correctly flagged the SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH + SLSA gaps but did not surface this one.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:25:50.815563Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:30:15.957020Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:30:15.956186Z","close_reason":"Landed in 2f25ea5d: release smoke now runs on Windows via pwsh and on all Unix release targets.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}

 {"id":"ft-yj375","title":"[security][P1] wa.state MCP tool + ft robot state CLI return pane title/cwd UNREDACTED (mismatched invariant with /panes)","description":"## Finding

Audit under ft-h8da2 (docs/security/read-path-redaction-matrix.md) confirms: wa.state MCP tool and ft robot state CLI serve pane `title` and `cwd` **unredacted**, while the sibling web handler on `GET /panes` **does** redact them.

### Evidence

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_types.rs:817-834 — McpPaneState::from_pane_info:

      title: info.title.clone(),
      cwd: info.cwd.clone(),

- crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:5501-5518 — PaneState::from_pane_info (CLI): same pattern, same unredacted clone.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/handlers.rs:64-70 — PaneView::from_record (web): **CORRECT** reference implementation:

      title: r.title.map(|t| redactor.redact(&t)),
      cwd: r.cwd.map(|c| redactor.redact(&c)),

### Threat

Pane titles are set by whatever process the pane hosts. Agents that bake credentials into the title (e.g. `codex --api-key sk-ant-...` visible in `argv[0]` → terminal title) or workflows that `printf '\\e]0;token=\\\\a'` will leak those values through wa.state and ft robot state.

A peer MCP agent that calls wa.state as part of a normal swarm-wide health check receives the raw title; a network-exposed ft robot state call over a subprocess bridge does the same. Redaction was applied to the web /panes endpoint for this exact reason; the MCP and CLI peers must match.

### Fix

Two equivalent options:

Option A (one-line per call-site, web-matching): in the wa.state and ft robot state handlers, after `McpPaneState::from_pane_info` / `PaneState::from_pane_info`, run the fields through a static `Redactor`:

    let redactor = Redactor::new();
    for s in &mut states {
        if let Some(t) = s.title.as_mut() { *t = redactor.redact(t); }
        if let Some(c) = s.cwd.as_mut()   { *c = redactor.redact(c); }
    }

Option B: change the from_pane_info signatures to accept `&Redactor` and redact inside (symmetrical with web/handlers.rs:64-70).

Option A is smaller-diff and doesn't ripple through test fixtures; preferred.

### Acceptance Criteria

- wa.state envelope serializes title/cwd through Redactor::redact.
- ft robot state envelope serializes title/cwd through Redactor::redact.
- Regression test: seed a pane with title containing `sk-ant-api03-XXXX`, invoke both surfaces, assert the raw token does not appear in the response JSON.
- docs/security/read-path-redaction-matrix.md wa.state + ft robot state rows flip from `✗ LEAK` to `✓`.

### Why P1

Real leak on the default configuration — no operator opt-in required. Blast radius is narrow (titles/cwds, not pane scrollback) but automatic (fleet-wide state poll). Fix is mechanical.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow while landing ft-h8da2's audit matrix, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:28:41.743939Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:40:33.038204Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:40:33.037749Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 4d26269f: wa.state and ft robot state redact title/cwd before serialization","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","cli","mcp","redaction","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-yjt9e","title":"policy::RateLimiter::pane_counts leaks entries forever — no eviction on pane destroy or GC path","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs:2076` — `RateLimiter.pane_counts: HashMap<(u64, ActionKind), Vec<Instant>>`. Every unique `(pane_id, action)` pair `check()` sees creates an entry. Nothing removes them.

A grep confirms it:

    $ rg 'pane_counts\.(remove|clear|retain)|fn gc|fn cleanup|fn prune_panes' crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs
    (no matches)

## Failure shape

On a long-running ft watcher with high pane churn (the 200+-pane fleet the project is designed for), every closed pane leaves a stale `(pane_id, action)` entry in `pane_counts` — one per ActionKind the pane ever touched. Over weeks those entries accumulate:

- Each entry holds a `Vec<Instant>` of up to `limit_per_pane` entries (default 60, configurable up to u32::MAX).
- After the sliding window expires, `prune_old` empties the Vec on the next `check()` — but only if check() is called with that (pane_id, action) again, which never happens for a destroyed pane.
- The entry itself is never removed, so the HashMap grows monotonically.

For 10k destroyed panes × 5 action kinds × 16B-per-Instant × 60-entries-peak = ~48 MB of leaked state. Not a crash, but a silent drift that scales with fleet uptime.

Global side (`global_counts: HashMap<ActionKind, Vec<Instant>>`) is BOUNDED by the finite ActionKind enum — not affected.

# Fix

Add two method shapes to `impl RateLimiter`:

1. `remove_pane(&mut self, pane_id: u64)` — iterate pane_counts, retain only keys whose first element ≠ pane_id. Called from the runtime's PaneDestroyed handler (same handler that already calls `remove_runtime_pane_state_for_pane` in runtime.rs:3574).

2. `gc(&mut self)` (or `gc_at(&mut self, now: Instant)` for testability) — iterate pane_counts, for each entry call prune_old with now-window as cutoff, drop the whole entry if the resulting Vec is empty. Safe opportunistic cleanup that catches panes closed without an explicit PaneDestroyed signal (crashes, abrupt detaches).

Also add operational telemetry: `pane_entries_evicted: u64` counter so operators can see the leak being managed.

# Tests

Inline `#[cfg(test)]` in policy.rs:

- `rate_limiter_remove_pane_drops_all_action_entries_for_that_pane`
- `rate_limiter_remove_pane_leaves_other_panes_intact`
- `rate_limiter_gc_drops_expired_empty_entries`
- `rate_limiter_gc_preserves_still-active_entries`

# Severity

MEDIUM — leak is slow, bounded per pane, doesn't panic. But it's cumulative and silent. Fleet operators would only see the symptom as gradual RSS creep over uptime-weeks, attributed to anything-but-rate-limiter. Shipping the eviction path closes the hole before someone has to bisect it from production.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:51:26.703056Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:57:11.104139Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:57:11.103827Z","close_reason":"Landed as part of 27ef45e1 (absorbed commit includes ft-yjt9e methods: RateLimiter::remove_pane, gc, gc_at, tracked_pane_entry_count + 4 regression tests: remove_pane_drops_all_action_entries_for_that_pane, remove_pane_leaves_global_counts_intact, gc_drops_expired_empty_entries, gc_preserves_still_active_entries). Lib compiles clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-yllta","title":"[MEDIUM] ft robot agents silently feature-gated behind 'agent-detection' — --no-default-features builds get robot.feature_not_available","description":"Reality-check audit F2: main.rs:21715 gates the whole agents family on agent_correlator::filesystem_detection_available() which returns cfg!(feature = "agent-detection"). @@ -0,0 +1,41 @@
+[package]
+name = "frankenterm-core-replay-types"
+description = "Leaf crate of replay-cluster types that frankenterm-core depends on (ft-j1qjt.1 — prerequisite for ft-j1qjt.2 replay-proper extraction)"
+version.workspace = true
+edition.workspace = true
+rust-version.workspace = true
+authors.workspace = true
+license-file.workspace = true
+repository.workspace = true
+
+[lints]
+workspace = true
+
+# Leaf crate carved out below frankenterm-core to break the
+# `core → replay` back-edge in `replay_decision_graph` (ft-j1qjt.1).
+# The canonical home of `DecisionType`, `DecisionEvent`, `DecisionGraph`,
+# `DecisionNode`, `CausalEdge`, and `EdgeType` — types that
+# `frankenterm-core::replay_capture` re-exports and that
+# `policy.rs` / `runtime.rs` / `workflows/runner.rs` reach into via
+# `crate::replay_capture::DecisionEvent::new(...)` etc.
+#
+# Same pattern as:
+#   - ft-usvnt: `frankenterm-core-resource-types` (backpressure types)
+#   - ft-g6sa8: `frankenterm-core-error-types` (error_codes)
+#   - ft-cf*: `frankenterm-core-config-types`
+#
+# The extracted module had ZERO `crate::*` dependencies — it was already
+# leaf-clean but was buried inside the core monolith. Carving it out
+# breaks one of the two `core → replay` edges identified in
+# docs/proposals/ft-j1qjt-replay-tier1-blocker.md (the
+# `DecisionEvent`/`DecisionType` references). The remaining edge —
+# `replay_capture::{SharedCaptureAdapter, CaptureAdapter, CaptureConfig,
+# CollectingCaptureSink}` — needs `RecorderEventSource` extracted from
+# `recording.rs` first; filed as ft-j1qjt.1.1.
+
+[dependencies]
+serde = { workspace = true }
+serde_json = { workspace = true }
+
+[dev-dependencies]
+proptest = "1.8.0"

@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+//! `frankenterm-core-replay-types` — Leaf crate of replay-cluster types
+//! that `frankenterm-core` depends on (ft-j1qjt.1).
+//!
+//! Carved out of `frankenterm-core` to break one of the two `core → replay`
+//! back-edges identified in
+//! `docs/proposals/ft-j1qjt-replay-tier1-blocker.md`. The
+//! `DecisionType` / `DecisionEvent` types live here so the future
+//! `frankenterm-core-replay` extraction (ft-j1qjt.2) does not need to
+//! pull `replay_decision_graph` along with the rest of the cluster.
+//!
+//! ## Dependency direction
+//!
+//! Leaf — depends only on `serde` + `serde_json`. ZERO `frankenterm-core`
+//! deps. Both `frankenterm-core` and the future
+//! `frankenterm-core-replay` will depend on this crate; no cycle.
+//!
+//! ## What's NOT here
+//!
+//! The remaining `core → replay` edge — `replay_capture::CaptureAdapter`
+//! and its inner `RecorderEventSource` field — is filed as a follow-up
+//! bead (ft-j1qjt.1.1). That extraction needs `RecorderEventSource`
+//! pulled out of `recording.rs` first, which is its own surgery.
+
+pub mod replay_decision_graph;
